verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase. It is anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, placing its kinase domain and SH2/SH3 domains in the cytoplasm. It has no extracellular domain and no segment that reaches the outer leaflet. It associates with cytoplasmic tails of surface receptors (e.g., BCR, FcεRI) but this is cytoplasmic and does not expose any portion to the extracellular face. No documented outer-leaflet or extracellular surface form exists.
